properties.name: Saya and the Dragon
properties.notes: Fantasy road-trip con story centered on Saya and an ancient dragon, with a more stable and consistent worldbuilding corpus than the Emily project.

properties.name: Saya
properties.role: protagonist / con artist / ex-temple prostitute / fake virgin sacrifice specialist / reluctant rescuer
properties.notes: Beautiful, witty, promiscuous, low-born survivor from Seebulba; protagonist, con artist, fake virgin-sacrifice specialist, thief, performer, and runaway branded property. Her adult swagger is built over a brutal childhood: possible maternal abandonment in a market, no known father, half-raised by street prostitutes, sold/forced into the Temple of the Bleeding Heart as an indentured girl, trained and disciplined as a temple prostitute, branded, pierced, licensed, medically certified, transferred through pleasure-house economies, and shaped further by Madam Zoobaya's luxury coercion machine. She is barely literate, speaks Seebulban fluently in dockside slang, knows sexual and tavern phrases across other languages, and learned survival through pickpocketing, seduction, lying, running, biting, swimming, rooftops, and reading danger in men before she can read a proper scroll. She jokes constantly about the past because direct pain frightens her; property, paperwork, brands, contracts, collars, elegant captivity, and rescue fantasies remain deep triggers. She mispronounces complex words, malapropiates formal terms, and is both shamelessly vulgar and secretly hungry to be remembered as more than a disposable dockside whore. Later arcs show her moral contradictions sharpening: she can steal, sell, con, and manipulate, yet repeatedly frees other owned women, recognizes branded girls, rescues Loma, and reacts violently to anyone being reduced to property.

properties.name: The Dragon
properties.role: deuteragonist / ancient dragon / partner in crime / frightened elder of a terrible brood
properties.notes: Ancient, sarcastic, gay, arthritic, sophisticated, fussy, hypochondriac dragon. Hoards treasure, fears aging and death, and reluctantly becomes Saya's closest ally and family-like companion. Later material stresses his horror at claymancy, his knowledge of dragon-blood harvesting as institutionalized agony, and plot-relevant physical frailty including arthritic pain and wing-joint stiffness; he temporarily ruptures with Saya over blood betrayal before reconciling. Extended canon: His cowardice and hypochondria are not fake; they are survival habits of something ancient enough to know how badly legends end. He hoards because treasure is proof he existed. He is pompous, gay, poetic, jealous, classically draconic, and often morally worse than Saya, yet becomes her shelter, witness, and home. He fears the Sisterhood, his Family, Threxaval, antimony, heroic idiots, aging joints, winter hibernation, and emotional sincerity.

properties.name: Saya and the Dragon
properties.kind: co-dependent partnership / road-trip scam alliance / chosen-family bond under strain
properties.notes: Starts as opportunistic collaboration around fake-sacrifice cons and becomes the emotional core of the story: banter, need, jealousy, protection, and chosen-family energy. Later arcs add a major rupture when Saya is willing to instrumentalize the Dragon's body through dragon-blood curiosity, followed by explicit apology, separation, and reconciliation rather than a simple reset. Expanded backstory layer: the Dragon becomes the first durable witness to Saya’s full history—brands, sale, temple discipline, Zoobaya, opium, paperwork terror, and fear of being reclaimed. His protectiveness is strongest when old ownership systems reappear.

properties.name: The Inner Sea
properties.notes: Shared basin of civilization, piracy, trade, war, plague, fashion, and contact between cultures.

properties.name: Longland
properties.notes: Warm fertile southern shore of the Inner Sea; politically fragmented into rival city-states.

properties.name: The Redwall Mountains
properties.notes: Massive reddish mountain chain separating Longland from northern regions.

properties.name: The Baronies
properties.notes: Cold fragmented zone of petty violence north of Longland.

properties.name: Hanigalbat
properties.notes: Dry rocky warning-zone beyond the Baronies where civilization thins into survival.

properties.name: The Upper Steppe
properties.notes: Mobile power zone of steppe conflict and periodic catastrophe.

properties.name: The Wall (Ruins)
properties.notes: Collapsed former alliance wall marking collective failure rather than true border.

properties.name: Lycan Lands
properties.notes: Working wilderness of timber routes, hereditary lycans, class fracture, silver violence, and ideological cosplay versus survival reality.

properties.name: Tanagra
properties.notes: Canonical spelling. Ancient refined Longland city-state at the meeting of the great river and the Redwall Mountains; politically central to Loma, Keldor, Ilma, Higara, House of Mara, and Ororo material.

properties.name: Seebulba
properties.notes: Major port city-state and Saya's origin: filthy, loud, salt-stinking, mercantile, improvisational, and spiritually oriented around appeasing the Old Deep One rather than being noticed by clean gods. It formed Saya through docks, canals, brothels, licensing offices, street prostitution, temple-brothel institutions, harbor debt, aggressive seafood/spice culture, mixed coinage, and brutal pragmatic survival. Saya defends it fiercely despite hating what it did to her: cats are workforce, rats and squid are food, literacy is low, dockside slang is real language, and everyone else looks down on Seebulba because they cannot survive it.

properties.name: The Longland City-States
properties.notes: Rival city-states whose cooperation is temporary, geographic, and self-interested.

properties.name: The Sisterhood of the Red Dawn
properties.notes: Militant ideological Amazon force north of Tanagra. Organized in banners under Virelda Sun-Clad. Locals call them the Scourge of the Upper Plains.

properties.name: Virelda Sun-Clad
properties.notes: Leader of the Sisterhood; charismatic tyrant claiming visions from the Mother Flame.

properties.name: Princess Loma of Tanagra
properties.notes: Canonical spelling updated to Tanagra. Eldest surviving daughter of King Keldor and Queen Ilma; legitimate heir by custom, raised ornamental and politically unprepared. Later material has her imprisoned in Madrigal and then confined at the House of the Veiled Ember, stripped of name and status as 'Novice Third Row', reinforcing how often others try to store, trade, or manage her politically. Extended canon: Loma evolves from ornamental fallen princess into Saya's recurring karmic burden, erotic confidante, hostage-business partner, and almost-sister. She is naive, entitled, sensual, repressed, easily panicked, sometimes ridiculous, but also increasingly durable. She is barely literate, has good-girl-gone-bad energy, carries the House of Mara curse, and attracts political, divine, marital, and predatory attention.

properties.name: King Keldor of Tanagra
properties.notes: Canonical spelling updated to Tanagra. Hedonistic ruler whose negligence helps trigger the palace succession catastrophe.

properties.name: Queen Ilma
properties.notes: Legendary mother of Loma from the cursed House of Mara; destroyed Yadwiga's sanctuary and left behind myth, curse, and pressure.

properties.name: Princess Higara
properties.notes: Younger daughter of Keldor by another woman; politically lethal, terroristic, and the direct anti-Loma. Later material links her more concretely to claymancy and associated atrocities.

properties.name: The Pox Girl
properties.notes: Slave girl Thrixa used in the conspiracy that kills King Keldor via venereal plague.

properties.name: Axiarthanax
properties.notes: Sleeker younger dragon whose claim over Saya triggers jealousy and aerial conflict.

properties.name: Ogden
properties.role: paladin / fake dragonslayer / himbo complication / swindler / recurring love-hate disaster
properties.notes: Associated with betrayal, romance-fragility, opportunism, and sharp emotional fallout in later arc summaries. Chapters from the Madrigal / siege material reinforce him as the on-page name most commonly used for the recurring man tied to Loma and Saya, including participation in escape material. Extended canon: Ogden/Odran is smug, beautiful, theatrical, occasionally brave, often selfish, and repeatedly disastrous. He drugs and robs Saya and the Dragon, betrays Loma with milkmaids, fakes his own death, becomes entangled with elven vengeance, and is emotionally useful because Saya can hate him more easily than miss him.
properties.canonical_name_note: Preferred surface name in later material for the same character also tracked as Sir Odran.

properties.name: Sir Odran
properties.notes: Recurring knightly / romantic complication figure. Current material continues to support treating Sir Odran and Ogden as the same recurring man via aliasing even when the surface-name usage skews strongly toward Ogden in later chapters.
properties.canonical_name_note: Alias of Ogden; later material favors the Ogden surface name while preserving same-character identity.

properties.name: The Cosmology
properties.content: Competing world-pictures involving flat-world or tray-world logic, giant snake foundations, layered heavens and hells, and multiple zodiac systems treated as lived belief rather than settled science.

properties.name: Road-trip con arc
properties.content: Recurring fake-sacrifice scam structure: Dragon terrorizes settlement, Saya infiltrates or poses as sacrifice, hero interference is manipulated, loot extracted, bond deepens.

properties.name: The Dwarf
properties.notes: Drunken dwarf Saya repeatedly encounters in inns; occasional hazy sexual-history joke and low-level recurring familiarity.

properties.name: Gregory the Demon
properties.role: lust demon / ex-lover / chaos contractor / goat-debt running gag
properties.notes: On-and-off lover and trouble source from Saya's brothel-era past. Trickster energy, strong sexual chemistry, and the recurring goat debt joke. Extended canon: Gregory is a minor hellspawn whose relationship with Saya is carnal, funny, mutually sleazy, and surprisingly honest. He offers demonic services, gets cursed into goat and mushroom forms, tempts domestic and infernal chaos, and functions as a portal to Hell's bureaucracy without ever becoming a stable partner.

properties.name: Sabrabena
properties.notes: Wealthier, softer, more perfumed estuary city in Longland.

properties.name: Delvida
properties.notes: More political and aristocratic estuary city opposite Sabrabena.

properties.name: Toemacha
properties.notes: Important port with somewhat divergent language and culture.

properties.name: Thelveth
properties.notes: Southern Longland city where the climate and temperament harden toward arid lands.

properties.name: Iolica
properties.notes: Canonical spelling updated to Iolica. Inland Longland administrative and trade-routing center; useful counterweight to the port-city texture.

properties.name: Lerida
properties.notes: Strategic city controlling northern access to the Inner Sea; tied to counterfeit-silver issues in Lycan Lands material.

properties.name: Remnant Empire
properties.notes: Ancient shrinking empire across the Inner Sea, surviving on memory, ritual, grandeur, and mercenaries.

properties.name: Aurigena
properties.notes: Gold-Born capital of the Remnant Empire; overbuilt, magnificent, and politically stagnant.

properties.name: Beyond the Empire
properties.notes: Known to exist but poorly understood and intentionally undefined.

properties.name: Elven Kingdoms
properties.notes: Collection of remote elven polities with radically distinct atmospheres and practices.

properties.name: Velaromíel
properties.notes: The Grove; oldest kingdom, deeply forested, bound to tree-spirits older than time.

properties.name: Nyssalaithe
properties.notes: The Shroud; mist-hidden mountain realm of death rites and ghost-summoning.

properties.name: Ethuradien
properties.notes: The Glass Spire; salt-desert city of impossible towers, optics, divination, and mind-mirrors.

properties.name: Caldrithalen
properties.notes: The Bleeding Vale; crimson cliffs, waterfalls, skin-runes, and open-blood magic.

properties.name: Tirae Sanúr
properties.notes: The Silver Marches; tundra realm of giant white elk and moon-reflecting towers.

properties.name: Lunavaleth
properties.notes: The Petal Court; sealed caldera jungle realm ruled by queens.

properties.name: Orraquíneth
properties.notes: The Gloam; subterranean fungal forest kingdom, whispered and half-doubted.

properties.name: Tangara
properties.notes: Legacy spelling retained only for historical graph continuity. Canonical current spelling is Tanagra.

properties.name: House of Mara
properties.notes: Maternal line tied to Queen Ilma and Loma, carrying multi-generational divine curse through female heirs.

properties.name: Yadwiga
properties.notes: Divine figure whose sanctuary at Mupa was destroyed by Queen Ilma, leaving lingering mythic consequences.

properties.name: Mupa
properties.notes: Sanctuary of Yadwiga, destroyed in Ilma's campaign.

properties.name: Archduke Severin the Pale
properties.notes: Luxurious predatory aristocrat tied to Loma's later harem arc; offers status and enclosure, then reveals fangs beneath glamour.

properties.name: Saya and the Dragon
properties.notes: Relationship note capturing the stable emotional truth: opportunistic alliance becoming home, irritation, protection, and reliance.

properties.name: Saya and Sir Odran
properties.notes: Mutual fraud, attraction, humiliation, cheating, and recurring bad judgment. Covers Ogden material too. His forged indenture papers and concubine proposal hit the deepest part of Saya’s backstory: the fear that desire, romance, and paperwork can turn her back into property.

properties.name: Saya and Loma
properties.notes: Mix of rivalry, pity, jealousy, blackmail, tenderness, sexual closeness, shared damage, and unstable family-like attachment. Saya recognizes in Loma another woman treated as movable property, though cushioned by royalty rather than docks. Their bond grows from exploitation and mockery into karmic guardianship: Saya keeps rescuing Loma partly from guilt, partly from rage at systems that sell girls, and partly because Loma becomes one of the few people who makes Saya’s buried caretaking instincts visible.

properties.name: Loma and Ogden
properties.notes: Loma reaches toward him as possibility and is repaid with fragility, selfishness, and collapse.

properties.name: Dragon and the Sisterhood
properties.notes: The Sisterhood is one of the few forces the Dragon genuinely fears; they cast him as the Patriarch Serpent and know how to make him strategic.

properties.name: Goddess Ororo
properties.role: agrarian goddess / patron-judge of House of Mara
properties.notes: Minor but real goddess associated with black soil, plows, harvest, judgment, and the House of Mara line. Watches over Loma/Tangara material in some traditions and appears in judging visionary scenes. Later material makes her more direct and accusatory toward Saya, pressing her over abandonment and responsibility.

properties.name: Old Rootbeard
properties.notes: Ancient ent-like authority figure leading angry tree-beings after the Dragon damages sacred woodland.

properties.name: The Blind Guardian
properties.notes: Ancient guardian of sacred waters whose death and consumption trigger Saya's contamination arc.

properties.name: Vaelthryx
properties.notes: Ancient dragon-name appearing both as the Dragon's old male fling and as an oath-level legendary reference in broader language.

properties.name: The First Mother
properties.role: foundational myth figure of the Sisterhood
properties.notes: Origin figure who melted her chains in fire and forged them into swords; ideological mother of the Sisterhood.

properties.name: Mother Flame
properties.content: Abstract purifying principle in Sisterhood belief: discipline, war, purification, rebirth through destruction.

properties.name: Oleander of Gibonia
properties.notes: Refined, self-consciously sophisticated romantic/erotic figure who seduces through verse, performance, and literary softness more than brute confidence.

properties.name: Gibonia
properties.notes: Place-identity attached to Oleander; currently light world detail, stronger as a label than as a mapped region.

properties.name: Aunt Threxaval the Bone-Eater
properties.role: draconic elder / terrifying aunt / ancestral authority
properties.notes: Ancient severe dragon elder associated with blood rites, excommunication, family judgment, and bone-throne-level ceremonial terror.

properties.name: Varnithax
properties.notes: Dead uncle haunting the Dragon's lineage with ultimatums, shame, worthiness tests, and cyclical deadline theatrics.

properties.name: Hedge Wizard
properties.role: recurring minor magic-user / local occult figure
properties.notes: Secondary wizard figure in the wider road-world: provincial, magical, and useful as part of the setting's low-to-mid-tier sorcery ecosystem rather than grand cosmological authority. Extended canon: Cheap, drunk, licensed, half-mad, and sometimes effective. The Dragon and Saya use him because reputable wizards are too expensive and too judgmental. He often thinks they are hallucinations and lives with unseen Maud.

properties.name: Swamp Hag
properties.role: recurring witch / hag / dangerous practical magic authority
properties.notes: Swamp-based occult figure tied to curses, remedies, body-problems, and chaotic but often useful intervention. Functions as one of the story's recurring practical-witch authorities. Extended canon: The Hag is more than a comic healer; she may be an ancient goddess-level swamp authority. She cures magical STDs, handles body-horror consequences, bargains for dragon relics, gives filthy practical advice, and may intervene in Saya's contamination dreams.

properties.name: Lombasa
properties.notes: Grimy coastal market city associated with harbor commerce, brothels, alchemists, and quick monetization of exotic vice goods. In frog-arc material, Saya sells hallucinogenic frogs here to buyers and rival brothels.

properties.name: Urveth
properties.notes: City identified through the cursed Library of Urveth. Strongly associated with demonic scholarship, clerical authority, forbidden texts, and psychic danger rather than civic detail.

properties.name: Garmor the Well Hung
properties.role: recent/WIP legendary hero / rumor-object of Saya's curiosity
properties.notes: Recent mega-arc / WIP figure introduced through tavern and market legend. Framed as a near-mythic heroic himbo with heavy sexual reputation: broad, confident, generous, and socially real as rumor before he is fully real as biography. Saya becomes immediately fascinated and pursues him under fake scholarly excuses; later material includes an actual encounter rather than rumor alone, but he should still be treated as somewhat soft/WIP compared with core recurring cast.

properties.name: Rugrun the Gruff
properties.role: ancestral founder-hero / House Mara legitimacy anchor
properties.notes: Legendary founder figure of House Mara. In recent Iolica/Iolika heist material, his sacred nose ring functions as a proof-of-lineage relic for Loma's dynastic claim. He is remembered as large enough that the ceremonial ring is absurdly huge, more like monumental bronze hardware than ordinary jewelry. He matters less as intimate character than as ancestral legitimacy-engine, showing how dynastic proof in this world depends on theatrical relics and bureaucratic absurdity.

properties.title: Sacred nose ring of Rugrun the Gruff
properties.summary: Ceremonial bronze lineage-relic of House Mara tied to Rugrun the Gruff. In the Iolica heist material it proves comically enormous—cart-wheel scale—and becomes the target of Saya and Loma's failed heist before being lost in the river.

properties.name: The Aethrafax Brothers
properties.role: conjoined rival-dragon brothers / published arc threat
properties.notes: Two-headed black dragon pair sharing one body. One head is spectacularly stupid, gluttonous, and easy to manipulate; the other is highly intelligent, strategic, and enraged at permanent fusion to an idiot. Killed in the altar-village arc after Saya is used as bait and the Dragon crushes the smarter head with a boulder.

properties.name: The Hermit of the Karma Hill
properties.notes: Cranky desert-hill hermit consulted by Saya in the bad-karma arc. Mocking, misogynistic, unhelpful, and occasionally truthful. Associated with a foul-mouthed crow and with vague suggestions that Saya may indeed be carrying some old curse.

properties.name: The Oracle of Holy Embroidery
properties.notes: Spiritual encounter-authority from the karma arc. Associated with holy embroidery aesthetics and golden llamas. Accuses Saya of devouring a sacred Mogwai, then banishes her without useful help, deepening the arc's panic and absurdity.

properties.name: Madam Zoobaya
properties.notes: Luxury-facing Seebulban pleasure-house madam, trafficker-manager, and formative backstory antagonist. Zoobaya markets chains, punishments, themes, correction, exotic intoxicants, and eroticized discipline as refinement or empowerment, making coercion look expensive and desirable. She shaped Saya's relationship to sex work, spectacle, luxury, debt, vice goods, and punishment: girls could be moved through gambling, debt-transfer, licensing, gift economies, and fashionable captivity. Zoobaya punished unauthorized use of premium drugs or stolen sweets, controlled access to hallucinogens and aphrodisiacs, and presided over a house where rivalry, accusation, and collective punishment converted vulnerable girls into competitors. Saya's adult mixture of professional pride, class resentment, performance skill, distrust of elegant cages, and hatred of being owned owes much to Zoobaya.

properties.name: Temple of the Bleeding Heart
properties.notes: Seebulban temple-brothel training institution that receives girls through court order, guard sweeps, debt, indenture, donation shortfalls, or outright trafficking. It fuses spiritual branding, erotic instruction, discipline, literacy theater, ritual performance, medical inspection, licensing, punishment, debt accounting, and sexual commerce. Saya's teenage years there define her central wound: she was told degradation was sacred education, property was vocation, and obedience was purity. She learned dance, seduction, performance, music, client-reading, hiding places, pain-management, escape habits, and contempt for institutions that call cages 'training'.

properties.name: Madam Zoobaya's Pleasure House
properties.kind: luxury pleasure house / coercive erotic complex
properties.notes: Luxury pleasure house / coercive erotic complex associated with naming variants such as House of Eternal Moans, House of Eternal Delights, House of Silk, and Emporium of Educated Delight. Its sensory world is velvet, baths, perfume, mirrors, chains, polished floors, themed punishments, glitter, vice goods, moon cakes, controlled intoxicants, eunuch guards, and staged refinement masking debt and ownership. For Saya it is neither simple victimhood nor nostalgia: she learned saleable skill there, suffered discipline there, stole there, competed there, and absorbed a poisonous model of beauty as both armor and leash.

properties.name: Looxa
properties.role: secondary house girl / Zoobaya-house social rival
properties.notes: Secondary Zoobaya-house girl remembered through accusation and rivalry scenes inside the house's punishment economy. Observant, reactive, and willing to accuse when collective punishment turns girls against one another. She matters mainly as evidence of how Zoobaya's system converts shared vulnerability into competition and betrayal.

properties.name: Gawa
properties.notes: Secondary worker figure from tavern / street-sex-work scraps. Pragmatic, bruised, sharp, and grounded; produces immediate worker-to-worker recognition with Saya. Useful as texture for the world's low-class solidarity layer rather than as a major recurring independent plot mover.

properties.name: Material Culture of the Inner Sea World
properties.content: Reference node for the setting's lived material culture: food, clothing, ornament, vessels, baths, incense, trade goods, and the overlap between luxury, ritual, vice, and coercion.

properties.name: Inner Sea food culture
properties.content: Recurring food texture includes seafood, goat and sheep products, bread and flatbread, olives, chickpeas, onions, peppers, figs, nuts, honey, wine, rough ale, pastries, and regional specialties such as pumpkin products in the Redwall zone.

properties.name: Longland clothing and adornment
properties.content: Baseline clothing system centers on linen tunics, silk wraps, wool cloaks, sandals or bare feet, bronze fittings, veils, belts, brooches, chains, anklets, toe rings, bangles, perfume, dyes, body paint, and presentation as class or role technology.

properties.name: Luxury, ritual, and coercion overlap
properties.content: Temples, brothels, dynastic houses, and pleasure complexes share a material vocabulary of veils, incense, chains, baths, velvet, mirrors, attendants, ritual display, and bodily control. Beauty often functions as infrastructure.

properties.name: Spice trade and spice culture
properties.content: Spices function as trade goods, class markers, market identity, vice-economy texture, and caravan/shipborne wealth. Repeated references include spice carts, spice stands, spice quarters, spice ships, spice syndicates, spice sacks, spice merchants, spiced chickpeas, spiced yogurt, spiced wine, spiced tea, and Delvida's spice-heavy cuisine. Seebulba is even framed in one chapter voice as Mistress of Spice and Sin.

properties.name: Spice trade and intoxicants
properties.content: Tracks recurring spice-market texture plus named substances: jalbaljah, ilanta leaves, gigglegrass, and heatgrass. Spices function as trade goods, class signals, cuisine markers, and vice-economy texture; some botanicals overlap with smoke blends and aphrodisiacs.

properties.name: Jalbaljah
properties.content: Named spice. The Dragon explicitly calls it aggressive even by dragon standards, implying an intensely hot or forceful spice used by humans in cuisine or blends.

properties.name: Ilanta leaves
properties.content: Flexible leaves used to roll smoke blends. Saya says ilanta leaves roll best when warm; skin heat and oils help keep them from cracking.

properties.name: Gigglegrass
properties.content: Recreational smoke blend / intoxicant. Saya steals a gigglegrass pouch, later rolls giggle grass in ilanta leaves, and smokes it socially.

properties.name: Heatgrass
properties.content: Dried botanical aphrodisiac or desire-amplifier. Specifically identified as amplifying suppressed desire rather than creating new desire. Velurian heatgrass is a named variety in the convent-aqueduct sabotage chapter.

properties.name: Chicka-chicka
properties.content: Named spice or seasoning from the Diving for Seafood chapter. Saya presents it as even more intense than jalbaljah; the Dragon says it is worse and should probably be regulated by priests. Children are said to eat it in Seebulban/coastal culture.

properties.name: Gingha leaves
properties.content: Leaves chewed after very hot food. Saya says they soothe the belly after harsh spices; they also turn the tongue purple, which she treats as a tavern flirtation trick.

properties.name: Madrigal
properties.kind: port city / siege city / mercantile harbor
properties.notes: Recurring port city with ancient sewers, harbor chains, fish-market and dungeon infrastructure, and a distinctly mercantile ethos of contracts and coin rather than holiness. Site of Loma's imprisonment, the clay warehouse discovery, and the later siege/recovery material.

properties.name: House of the Veiled Ember
properties.notes: Cold-storage disciplinary house on chalk cliffs above the Veil in the northern Baronies. Charcoal linen, enforced silence, renamed novices, barefoot frost, and punitive containment of inconvenient women; Loma is held here as 'Novice Third Row'.

properties.name: Claymancy
properties.notes: Forbidden art of binding something living or soul-like into clay constructs. Distinguished by the Dragon from ordinary pottery and treated as scalable, unnatural, and ethically horrifying.

properties.name: Dragon blood
properties.notes: Illicit alchemical product derived from harvested dragon suffering and sold as potent lust-enhancing substance. The Dragon describes it as carrying dangerous echoes of power, hunger, memory, and madness beyond ordinary aphrodisiacs.

properties.name: The Succubus
properties.notes: Recurring infernal figure tied to Saya's past and to Gregory the Demon. Flirtatious, transactional, and operationally competent; disrupts the House of the Veiled Ember and later reappears as an active agent rather than a one-off note. Extended canon: Freed by Saya from a coercive magic circle in Urveth, the Succubus treats Saya as a sister in sin and sometimes as a genuine friend. She is tied to infernal performance metrics, Gregory's network, decadent moonlit picnics, and the Veiled Ember sabotage.

properties.name: Bollo
properties.role: Taurean former regular / remembered lover from Saya's brothel years
properties.notes: Taurean brute strongly associated with Saya's genuinely fond sexual memory rather than coercive trauma. Remembered as huge, simple, earthy, physically satisfying, and emotionally uncomplicated; later material indicates a nostalgic reunion, while dream and masturbation material shows he remains one of Saya's persistent benchmarks for remembered bodily pleasure.

properties.name: Adora of Valhrona
properties.role: WIP dynastic captor figure / provisional later-arc actor
properties.notes: Currently supported only by WIP index material. Adora of Valhrona is identified as holding Loma in a dungeon while Higara of Tanagra wants Loma alive for separate political purposes. Keep as tracked but explicitly provisional until chapter text exists.

properties.name: Valhrona
properties.notes: Currently supported only by WIP index material via 'Adora of Valhrona.' Treat Valhrona as the current canonical spelling; 'Velhrona' should be treated as a likely variant or mistaken form until chapter text stabilizes the name.

properties.name: The Crow
properties.role: messenger / obscene familiar / recurring avian heckler
properties.notes: Messenger for the Swamp Hag and sometimes the Dragon’s ghostly Family deadlines. The Crow taunts Saya with crude comments, delivers ominous news, may be one of several similar crows, and is disposable enough that the Dragon has incinerated at least one.

properties.name: Maud
properties.role: unseen companion of the Hedge Wizard
properties.notes: Possibly real, possibly imaginary companion addressed by the Hedge Wizard in his crumbling tower. Useful as a texture node for his derangement and rural magic ecosystem.

properties.name: Sir Mibbs the Flame-Breaker
properties.role: goat-boy fake hero turned folk hero
properties.notes: Nineteen-year-old goat-boy Saya tries to manufacture into a dragonslayer. Cowardly, sweet, physically hopeless, and eventually rebrands a botched scam into a minor heroic legend.

properties.name: Grash
properties.role: gentle bridge troll / temporary domestic partner
properties.notes: Gentle troll who gives Saya warmth, bacon, sex, and domestic comfort until she panics at becoming happy and steals his toll pot.

properties.name: Elira
properties.notes: Girl rescued from witch-burning alongside Saya; revealed to be a bound demon after the Dragon destroys the zealot town.

properties.name: Gerald
properties.notes: Demon who once tried to buy Saya’s soul for mediocre perks. Saya negotiated a sexual favor instead and kept an enchanted cinnamon-scented match.

properties.name: Lady Maribel
properties.notes: Noblewoman trapped in an arcane chastity belt by a jealous husband. Saya fails to free her directly but helps turn the belt into a profitable kink niche, then quietly forfeits her cut.

properties.name: Lord Velimor
properties.notes: Besieger of holy Garthuun. Saya keeps him sweet while the Dragon is tempted by the Vault of Suleshan; his campaign ends in a nearly empty vault and humiliation.

properties.name: Lord Velgarth
properties.role: boar-obsessed warlord / accidental patron of Saya’s oracle scam
properties.notes: Kidnaps Saya after mistaking her prophetess con for real dragon-taming power. He seeks conquest and ends up receiving sex coaching before the Dragon and radicalized peasants ruin his campaign.

properties.name: Kholf
properties.notes: Barbarian who keeps Saya as a gold-chained bed-thrall in winter; attached to a sword he treats like a steel wife.

properties.name: Rikka
properties.notes: Harsh barbarian fiancée who receives Saya as a servant and judges her failed domestic labor.

properties.name: Lord Artag
properties.notes: Steppe lord who buys Saya as exotic nobility. She nurses him through illness in a warm tent and gains influence until the Dragon destroys the setup as a rescue.

properties.name: Captain Menos
properties.notes: Scarred captain Saya uses for sex and information while tracking Loma through Khoraz.

properties.name: Vael
properties.notes: Stylish initiate Lycan whose speeches seduce Saya intellectually and sexually before his cowardice during a baronial raid exposes revolution-as-performance.

properties.name: Rheva
properties.notes: Associate of Vael, part of the ideological Lycan set that romanticizes lycanthropy and flees when violence becomes materially dangerous.

properties.name: Adira
properties.role: traumatized Toemachan sex worker / vampire warning memory
properties.notes: Fellow worker from Saya’s youth who returned from a pale cold duke changed, half-white-haired and broken. Her fate fuels Saya’s fear of vampires.

properties.name: Captain Lyri
properties.notes: Amazon/Sisterhood captain connected to Saya’s indiscretion about the Dragon killing a sister, escalating Sisterhood danger.

properties.name: Master Tovin
properties.notes: Skeptical shoemaker whom Saya seduces for sky-blue strappy sandals while pretending to be a hidden centaur-raised princess.

properties.name: Rimok
properties.notes: Tax collector robbed during Saya’s Smoke & Mirrors village panic scam.

properties.name: Bess
properties.notes: One of Saya’s reckless flings during the Hay Fever period, almost exposing the scam.

properties.name: Torv
properties.notes: One of Saya’s reckless flings during the Hay Fever period, almost exposing the scam.

properties.name: Mira
properties.notes: One of two real virgins offered with Saya and cheese; freed by the Dragon rather than exploited.

properties.name: Lisha
properties.notes: One of two real virgins offered with Saya and cheese; freed by the Dragon rather than exploited.

properties.name: Beran / Bren
properties.role: rusty country lad / would-be dragon slayer
properties.notes: Naive local lad distracted by Saya during a sacrifice scam, then sent running to spread the story.

properties.name: The Gnome Prince
properties.notes: Small naked gnome prince released by Saya’s frog-kissing ritual; politely explains the logistical limits of being her prince.

properties.name: The Pregnant Elven Princess
properties.notes: Elven princess seeking Ogden for presumed defilement and pregnancy. Her war-band drags Saya into elven vengeance politics.

properties.name: Braddo
properties.notes: Man who took half of Saya’s earnings during her “Saya of the Cask” barrel-freelancing period behind the Crooked Spigot.

properties.name: Årff
properties.notes: Barn hookup used by Saya to blow off stress from Dragon family threats; pays her extra while she lies about being Duchess of Froufrou.

properties.name: The Dark-Elf Antiquarian
properties.notes: Shady dealer who trades Saya a fake water-wyrm scale for real dragon scales during the Threxaval scale crisis.

properties.name: The Archdeacon of Urveth
properties.notes: Keeps an unregistered Succubus trapped in a circle inside the Library of Urveth; signs Saya’s forged access certificate and reveals larval demonic corruption.

properties.name: The Fallen Angel Domesticator
properties.notes: The Succubus’s fallen angel lover whose nesting, poached eggs, hydration reminders, and exclusivity threaten her demonic career metrics.

properties.name: The Family / Prime Broods
properties.notes: Terrifying ancestral dragon structure that judges the Dragon for wasting his bloodline. Includes ghosts, aunts, sisters, etiquette, excommunication, and ritualized shame.

properties.name: First Red Banner
properties.notes: Elite shock troops and personal guard of the Matriarch-General.

properties.name: Second White Banner
properties.notes: Intelligence and ideological officers known as Sisters of Clarity.

properties.name: Third Blue Banner
properties.notes: Logistics, siege engineers, and indoctrination overseers.

properties.name: Fourth Black Banner
properties.notes: Liberators responsible for rounding up and sorting women after conquest.

properties.name: Fifth Green Banner
properties.notes: Supply, livestock, and captured workforce management.

properties.name: The Library of Urveth
properties.notes: Reality-warping library that eats minds, loops hallways, bleeds walls, and houses the Lexicon of Akheron. Literacy and study here can become body-horror.

properties.name: Lerida Authorities
properties.notes: Authorities who coerce Saya into duchess impersonation and assassination plots, and who control northern access to the Inner Sea.

properties.name: Baronial Patrols
properties.notes: Silver-armed patrols securing timber routes and punishing lycan villages after incidents; often paranoid, exhausted, and supplied with counterfeit silver.

properties.name: Native Mountain Lycans
properties.notes: Practical mountain families managing inherited transformation with chains, hooks, barred windows, timber work, and mistrust.

properties.name: Gentrified Initiate Lycans
properties.notes: Young noble radicals who frame lycanthropy as liberation and identity while locals experience it as biology and danger.

properties.name: Third Heaven
properties.notes: Heavenly administrative realm used in infernal/celestial paperwork; potentially capable of reclaiming a fallen angel as incorruptible.

properties.name: Hell Bureaucracy
properties.notes: Contractual, quota-driven, performance-metric afterlife machinery behind Gregory, the Succubus, Gerald, and demonic paperwork.

properties.name: Garthuun
properties.notes: Besieged holy city whose mythical Vault of Suleshan proves nearly empty due to embezzlement.

properties.name: Khoraz
properties.notes: Place where Saya beds Captain Menos while searching for news of Loma.

properties.name: Kishkh
properties.notes: Site where Loma’s purity is confirmed and wedding/alliance announced before siege pressure returns.

properties.name: Varn's Rise
properties.notes: Location of Ogden’s fake tomb and rhino-death story, later exposed as part of his death-faking chaos.

properties.name: The King's Road
properties.notes: Road where Saya and Dragon discuss Longland’s political geography and Toemacha pronunciation.

properties.name: Zarnathi Jungle
properties.notes: Vine-choked southern jungle containing humming temples, sacred pools, Blind Guardian contamination, and hallucinogenic frog commerce.

properties.name: Threxaval’s Crystal Caldera Sanctuary
properties.notes: Crystal-and-bone cathedral where Threxaval has petrified herself in eternal disgusted judgment.

properties.name: The Veil
properties.notes: Chalk cliff/coastal zone above which the House of the Veiled Ember confines inconvenient women.

properties.name: The Spice Islands
properties.notes: Threatened destination for unruly girls from the Temple of the Bleeding Heart; probably exaggerated with cannibal stories for discipline.

properties.name: The Crooked Spigot
properties.notes: Tavern with the giant wine barrel behind it where Saya freelanced as Saya of the Cask.

properties.name: The Goat's Elbow
properties.notes: Inn where Saya books a room after selling stolen armor and is interrupted by the same furious paladin.

properties.name: The Sloshed Satyr
properties.notes: Tavern where Saya publicly confronts Sir Odran, leading to hate-sex and further rivalry.

properties.name: Vault of Suleshan
properties.notes: Supposed obscene treasure hoard beneath holy Garthuun; nearly empty when finally reached.

properties.name: Inner Sea Ports
properties.notes: General network of vice harbors, spice markets, coinage systems, sailors, brothels, and rumor movement.

properties.name: Temple and Brothel Backrooms
properties.notes: Composite space of punishment rooms, baths, mirrored chambers, incense, legal coercion, and eroticized discipline.

properties.title: Lexicon of Akheron
properties.summary: Cursed grimoire in the Library of Urveth; a brain-melting, demonic text worth ten thousand gold sovereigns and a virginal goat in Gregory’s job offer.

properties.title: Phoenix-bone dagger
properties.summary: Valuable dagger stolen by Sir Odran during the Spoils of Lust betrayal.

properties.title: Priest-king’s sun medallion / Odran medallion
properties.summary: Recurring stolen ornament tied to Odran/Ogden and eventually pressed into dirt at his false/actual mourning site.

properties.title: Threxaval’s ancient tea cup
properties.summary: Still-full tea cup stolen by Saya as substitute payment to the Swamp Hag after Threxaval is found petrified.

properties.title: Enchanted cinnamon-scented match
properties.summary: Tip from Gerald the bureaucrat demon after Saya refuses to sell her soul.

properties.title: Lady Maribel’s arcane chastity belt
properties.summary: Zap-happy magical belt that becomes a profitable kink-market opportunity.

properties.title: Moon-Poon the Swan
properties.summary: Ceremonial swan stolen by Saya as a pet, eaten by a fox and avenged by fox-eating.

properties.title: Jar of premium hallucinogenic frogs
properties.summary: Captured Zarnathi frogs sold by Saya to Lombasa alchemists and brothels as premium vice goods.

properties.title: Loma’s self-replenishing samovar
properties.summary: Magical tower-prison comfort object supplying warmth and tea during Saya and Loma’s winter refuge.

properties.title: Vael’s iron wolf-head collar
properties.summary: Lycan revolutionary symbol Saya admires, then distrusts after the Dragon frames it as containment rather than freedom.

properties.title: Saya’s walking jewelry hoard
properties.summary: Bangles, anklets, hip chains, toe rings, and ornaments Saya treats as mobile wealth, armor, sex appeal, and defiance of former chains.

properties.title: Dragon saddle / no-glitter riding gear
properties.summary: Proposed riding solution after Saya complains about bareback dragon travel; Dragon grudgingly concedes only no glitter.

properties.name: Saya narrative voice
properties.content: First-person Saya narration should be bratty, vulgar, emotionally evasive, street-smart, and full of malapropisms for complex words. Her jokes should not erase fear, trauma, or real tenderness.

properties.name: Saya mispronounces complex words
properties.content: Saya routinely mangles uncommon or four-syllable words such as indentured and uses malapropisms. This is a character-voice rule, not mere spelling error.

properties.name: Saya forgets what prostitute means
properties.content: Recurring bit: Saya forgets or resists the word prostitute; the Dragon or others clarify that it means whore.

properties.name: Clothing rule: no socks or underwear
properties.content: World clothing convention: no one uses socks or underwear. Saya wears sandals, slippers, barefoot sandals, or goes barefoot; never boots unless explicitly overridden.

properties.name: Species metal allergies
properties.content: Elves are allergic to iron; lycans are allergic to silver; dragons are allergic to antimony for some reason. This is a useful practical magic/worldbuilding rule.

properties.name: Fake virgin sacrifice scam mechanics
properties.content: Core scheme: Dragon terrorizes a settlement; Saya infiltrates, manipulates tribute logic, offers herself as sacrifice or prophetess, distracts heroes, and they split loot while improvising around failures.

properties.name: Mobile hoard and heat-cycle logistics
properties.content: Saya and Dragon choose soft-target regions, stash loot in temporary caves or ruins, work a short-range con circuit, then relocate when suspicion rises. Lost hoards hurt the Dragon as identity wounds.

properties.name: Saya’s false biographies
properties.content: Saya invents noble, scholarly, priestess, harem, rebel, and royal pasts. Most are ridiculous covers for illiteracy, street survival, whorehouse trauma, petty theft, and failed domestic skills.

properties.name: Saya’s cooking incompetence
properties.content: Saya sincerely tries to cook but repeatedly creates disasters, poisons, burnt porridge, and emotional breakdowns; the Dragon is a far better cook.

properties.name: Saya’s brands, piercings, and lust rune
properties.content: Saya carries faded slavery brands, temple/brothel piercings, and a Morean rune she thought protected against disease/pregnancy but which actually sustains sexual arousal in whorehouse slaves.

properties.name: Saya’s practical skill profile
properties.content: Strong at stabbing with improvised small sharp objects, kicking, biting, running, jumping, swimming, pickpocketing, simple locks, urban escape, singing, dancing, and seduction. Bad at bows, polearms, swords, axes, healing, hunting, and cooking.

properties.name: Saya’s turnip trauma
properties.content: Saya has a passive fear and disdain for turnips rooted in childhood deprivation and humiliation.

properties.name: Dragon hypochondria and frailty
properties.content: The Dragon’s age, arthritis, gout, eyesight, wing stiffness, hibernation, and fear of fatal heroics make him cautious and comic without making him harmless.

properties.name: Dragon shapeshifting cost
properties.content: The Dragon can shapeshift but rarely does because it wrecks his body and makes him ache for days.

properties.name: Dragon food and diet taboos
properties.content: The Dragon eats enormous amounts, ranks human cuisines with disturbing politeness, avoids reptiles as kin, enjoys bone marrow, and treats sheep tribute as practical luxury.

properties.name: Dangerous divine attention
properties.content: Being noticed by gods or cosmic forces is both blessing and curse. Loma is cosmologically visible; Saya becomes visible by proximity and repeated threshold violations.

properties.name: Blind Guardian contamination arc
properties.content: Saya eats part of the Blind Guardian after bathing in a sacred pool and develops humming, sleep-whispers, glow-marks, dream-maze doubles, and uncertain metaphysical residue.

properties.name: Claymancy ethics
properties.content: Claymancy binds living or soul-like substance into clay bodies; the Dragon treats it as unnatural, scalable atrocity linked to Higara’s political horror.

properties.name: Dragon-blood trade ethics
properties.content: Dragon blood is not just a lust drug; it is suffering harvested from dragons, carrying power, hunger, memory, and madness. Saya’s curiosity about it damages her bond with the Dragon.

properties.name: Loma as Saya’s karmic charge
properties.content: Saya claims Loma is not her problem while repeatedly rescuing, exploiting, sleeping beside, corrupting, feeding, protecting, and tracking her. The Dragon identifies this as karmic guardianship.

properties.name: Loma purity politics
properties.content: Loma’s political value repeatedly depends on being considered pure, marriageable, legitimate, or symbolically unspoiled. Saya treats this with practical cynicism and magical restoration logistics.

properties.name: Loma good-girl-gone-bad dynamic
properties.content: Loma combines royal repression, hidden palace sexuality, fallen-princess need, and growing appetite for survival and pleasure.

properties.name: The Ogden problem
properties.content: Ogden/Odran is not just a lover or villain; he is a recurring masculine disaster engine whose vanity, beauty, cowardice, bravado, and betrayals keep creating plot consequences.

properties.name: Gregory’s goat debt
properties.content: Running joke and relationship marker: Gregory owes Saya at least one goat, possibly more depending on curses, failed bargains, and emotional damages.

properties.name: Saya and the Succubus sisterhood
properties.content: Saya’s bond with the Succubus is rooted in freeing her from sexual slavery; their decadent friendship is both comic and a mirror of survival solidarity.

properties.name: Sisterhood ideology
properties.content: The Sisterhood claims emancipation through annihilation: no sons, no masters, beauty as divine proof, labor as sacred, and pleasure subordinated to purpose. Saya is fascinated and horrified.

properties.name: Sisterhood banner system
properties.content: The Sisterhood is organized into color-coded banners: Red shock troops, White intelligence, Blue logistics/indoctrination, Black liberators, Green supply/workforce management.

properties.name: Lycan class fracture
properties.content: Native lycans manage inherited transformation as danger; initiate lycans romanticize voluntary turning as identity politics. Saya learns slogans do not stop silver arrows.

properties.name: Inner Sea coinage
properties.content: City-state coinage is inconsistent and political: Seebulban octopuses, Sabrabenan sardines, Delvidan dolphins, Toemachan lyres, Tanagran eyes, Thelveth blooms, Leridan shields, rare elven shimmer, and heavy dwarven slabs.

properties.name: Longland Zodiac: Twelve Beasts
properties.content: Common zodiac of Rat, Mule, Stray Dog, Goat, Crow, Eel, Ox, Fox, Boar, Horse, Wolf, Swan, with Toemacha replacing Swan with Goose.

properties.name: Sailor’s Zodiac: Twelve Turns of Fortune
properties.content: Alternative zodiac used by sailors, moneylenders, temple girls, and minor nobles: Broken Coin, Tied Maiden, Open Hand, Three-Legged Beast, Spilled Wine, Collar, Empty Bowl, Twin Fangs, Closed Gate, Drowned Lantern, Crooked Scale, Open Road.

properties.name: Heavens and Hells structure
properties.content: Longland cosmology has Mortal Coil, 9–12 bureaucratic heavens, at least 13 administrative/punitive/transformative hells, karma as weight/debt, and gods as real but petty jurisdictions.

properties.name: Sabrabena Heavenly Lamb cult
properties.content: Soft-control religion of purity, sacrifice, confession, cleansing, white veils, magistrates, and guided obedience rather than overt brutality.

properties.name: Aurigena Golden Calf cult
properties.content: Imperial/market religion of wealth, appetite, success, gold offerings, sacred markets, and inequality justified as failed favor.

properties.name: Yadwiga as plague and cleanliness goddess
properties.content: Yadwiga sends plague partly through divine anger over filth and improper washing; shrine culture links hygiene, punishment, and public health in comic theological form.

properties.name: Sports and games of the setting
properties.content: Barony football, foxtossing, mud pit fighting, grape pit brawling, dust arena scraps, pit fights, rooster fights, insect fights, hog riding, dice games, greased pole climbing, gauntlets, goat vault, wife carrying, pole fighting, kissing contests, and barony pole vaulting.

properties.name: Saya’s sports profile
properties.content: Saya is suited to dirty brawls, parkour, gauntlets, biting, thigh traps, cheating at dice, and weaponized kissing; she gambles recklessly and loses often.

properties.name: Brothel and temple economy
properties.content: Sex work, temple service, debt, branding, training, punishment, advertising, and spiritual language overlap. Zoobaya and the Temple of the Bleeding Heart turn coercion into luxury and ritual.

properties.name: Harem politics
properties.content: Harems function as silk-covered gladiator pits: chore sabotage, poison tea, hierarchy, erotic competition, status games, and constant surveillance.

properties.name: Saya’s vampire fear
properties.content: Saya’s fear of vampires is grounded in Adira’s youth trauma and Severin’s harem disaster: beautiful undead aristocrats turn desire into cold possession and erasure.

properties.name: Elves as dangerous hermit aristocracies
properties.content: Elves are beautiful, cruel, repressed, alien, and magically costly. Grove elf death squads leaving their kingdoms means something serious has broken.

properties.name: Taureans
properties.content: Large bull-like humanoids associated with Bollo and Saya’s fondness for earthy, physically overwhelming, emotionally uncomplicated lovers.

properties.name: Barony boys in Seebulban brothels
properties.content: Third sons and haystack knights enter Seebulba brothels promising rescue and glory, then vanish. Saya learned to keep coin instead of promises.

properties.name: Saya’s failed honest work
properties.content: Saya repeatedly tried honest labor—washing, fish stalls, bakery, grape work, house service, rat catching—and found it paid worse, exploited her body anyway, and punished her survival instincts.

properties.name: Seebulba cat policy
properties.content: Seebulba does not eat alley cats; cats are vermin-control workforce. Skewers are more likely dock rats or argumentative squid.

properties.name: Moonlight infernal picnics
properties.content: Decadent meetings with the Succubus include baklava, nargile, gossip, sex, and infernal problem-solving.

properties.name: Loma hostage-business arc
properties.content: Saya and Loma attempt recurring fake kidnappings and ransom letters, only to learn Loma’s relatives often treat her as liability rather than valuable hostage.

properties.name: Veiled Ember liberation arc
properties.content: Saya finds Loma silenced as Novice Third Row, rejects subtle rescue, uses the Succubus and Velurian heatgrass to collapse the convent’s repression into pandemonium.

properties.name: Oleander’s purity projection
properties.content: Oleander sees Saya as a saint disguised as sinner, which wounds her because he worships a possible self she fears will get him killed or prove false.

properties.name: Saya’s humiliating grief
properties.content: Saya’s tears over cooking failure, Ogden’s apparent death, Oleander’s belief, and Dragon distance show vulnerability she violently denies.

properties.name: Old world / new world seam theory
properties.content: The Dragon theorizes Saya and he stumbled onto a metaphysical fault line where the old world is dying and a new one is being born, explaining abnormal supernatural attention.

properties.name: State as monopoly of violence gag
properties.content: The Dragon explains a state as an organization claiming monopoly over violence; Saya briefly thinks her street-urchin gang qualified until reminded they got arrested.

properties.name: Mibbs fake-hero arc
properties.content: Saya manufactures a heroic brand around goat-boy Mibbs, fails spectacularly when he confesses the scam, then watches him become a minor legend anyway.

properties.name: Sisterhood capture and expulsion arc
properties.content: Saya is conscripted by the Sisterhood, endures shaving, marches, ideology, and punishment, writes an unsent letter to the Dragon, bites an officer, and is expelled into the wilderness before reunion.

properties.name: Library of Urveth arc
properties.content: Gregory hires Saya to steal the Lexicon of Akheron. The Library warps reality; Saya frees the Succubus, suffers mindworm terror, and learns some texts are not meant to be read.

properties.name: Threxaval scale debt arc
properties.content: Swamp Hag demands a scale from Aunt Threxaval. Saya and Dragon fail through forgery, contact Family, discover Threxaval petrified, and settle the debt with her ancient tea cup.

properties.name: Winter north and Loma tower arc
properties.content: The Dragon hibernates; Saya survives northern cold, cannibal sisters, tower-prison luxury with Loma, barbarian captivity, and Artag’s tent before the Dragon destroys her comfort as rescue.

properties.name: Archduke Severin vampire harem arc
properties.content: Saya infiltrates Severin’s harem through Loma, discovers vampiric wives and Severin’s cowardice, rescues Loma through garlic panic, and leaves Loma furious over lost luxury.

properties.name: Loma purity and marriage arc
properties.content: Loma returns worn and working, needs symbolic restoration for marriage politics; Saya brings her to the Hag, preserves her memory, and follows the ensuing siege and flight risk.

properties.name: Hallucinogenic frog vice-trade arc
properties.content: Saya captures premium frogs in southern jungle, sells them to Lombasa alchemists and brothels, then overreaches by painting fake frogs, triggering refund-policy violence.

properties.name: Lycan Lands ideological disillusionment arc
properties.content: Saya enters Lycan country chasing Ogden’s silver map, becomes dazzled by revolutionary rhetoric, sees native suffering and elite cowardice, and leaves with slogans punctured.

properties.name: Ogden elven vengeance arc
properties.content: Saya mourns Ogden, discovers he faked death to dodge a pregnant elven princess’s death squad, lies to elves, confronts him, and becomes accessory to his idiocy.

properties.name: House of the Veiled Ember arc
properties.content: Loma is stored in a punitive convent; Saya escalates from rescue planning to hormonal uprising with heatgrass and the Succubus.

properties.name: Oleander troubadour arc
properties.content: Oleander projects saintly purity onto Saya through bad poetry, unsettling her more than lust or insults; his unpoetic removal leaves unexpected silence.

properties.name: Oracle and Velgarth warlord arc
properties.content: Saya turns fortune-telling into a prophetess con, is kidnapped for state service, sex-coaches a warlord, and is rescued when the Dragon radicalizes peasants instead.

properties.name: Garthuun mercenary treasure arc
properties.content: Saya and Dragon join Velimor’s siege chasing the Vault of Suleshan; the Dragon burns the gate but the vault is nearly empty, exposing greed and institutional embezzlement.

properties.name: Saya and Gregory
properties.notes: Carnal, comic, mutually transactional and strangely affectionate bond organized around sex, goats owed, demonic jobs, curses, and shared lack of shame.

properties.name: Saya and the Succubus
properties.notes: Saya frees the Succubus from coercive sexual slavery; they develop decadent friendship, gossip, rescue logistics, and mutual recognition.

properties.name: Saya and the Dwarf
properties.notes: Recurring inn-world familiarity: drinking, gossip, commentary, vague sexual history neither fully remembers nor wants to examine.

properties.name: Saya and Bollo
properties.notes: Bollo remains Saya’s benchmark for uncomplicated physical pleasure and one of the few brothel memories framed with sincere warmth.

properties.name: Saya and Loma — extended
properties.kind: hostage partners / lovers / quasi-sisters / karmic charge
properties.notes: Saya exploits, teaches, corrupts, rescues, mocks, and protects Loma. Their bond is erotic, sisterly, transactional, and emotionally dangerous.

properties.name: Dragon and the Family
properties.notes: The Dragon’s Family embodies everything he fled: grandeur, cruelty, judgment, etiquette, and demands to reclaim draconic greatness.

properties.name: Saya and Ogden — extended
properties.notes: They rob, desire, betray, humiliate, and rescue-adjacent each other. Saya hates that he can still make her care.

properties.name: Loma and Higara
properties.notes: Higara has the political decisiveness and cruelty Loma lacks; Loma is a legitimate heir made vulnerable by upbringing and symbolism.

properties.name: Saya and Madam Zoobaya
properties.notes: Zoobaya’s coercive luxury system formed many of Saya’s skills, wounds, market instincts, and disgust with ownership.

properties.name: Saya and the Swamp Hag
properties.notes: The Hag heals, mocks, bargains with, and sometimes saves Saya from consequences of magical sex, contamination, and reckless decisions.

properties.name: Saya and Oleander
properties.notes: Oleander worships Saya as pure beneath filth; this hurts her because it sees something possible, not something safe.

properties.name: Saya and Vael
properties.notes: Vael gives expensive words to Saya’s lived exploitation, then exposes the hollowness of elite radicalism by fleeing violence.

properties.name: Saya and Lord Artag
properties.notes: Saya is bought as captive luxury but gains power by nursing Artag and managing his camp through illness.

properties.name: Loma and Severin
properties.notes: Loma sees Severin’s harem as status and luxurious refuge until vampirism and cowardice destroy the fantasy.

properties.name: Dragon, Saya, and Loma
properties.notes: The Dragon becomes reluctant witness, transport, judge, and shelter for Saya’s increasingly protective entanglement with Loma.

properties.name: Higara’s mother
properties.role: Toemachan street whore sold to Tanagran royal harem
properties.notes: Unnamed mother of Princess Higara. She was a street whore from Toemacha sold into King Keldor’s harem and became his favorite after Queen Ilma died, giving Higara a short but politically explosive branch of the royal genealogy.

properties.name: Saya backstory spine
properties.content: Composite background arc: market abandonment or early maternal loss; dockside street survival in Seebulba; half-raising by working women; forced sale/indenture into the Temple of the Bleeding Heart; training, branding, piercing, discipline, licensing, medical certification, and pleasure-house transfers; Zoobaya-era luxury coercion; escapes, suspicious fire, unlicensed work, opium numbness, honest-work failures, fear of legal recapture, and eventual transformation into the Dragon's partner in crime.
properties.source_basis: Synthesized from Lore chapters 34, 94, 98, 184, 193-201, 204, 221, 403, 415-417, 445-446, core project notes.

properties.name: Saya core wound: owned, abandoned, and misfiled by fate
properties.content: Saya’s deepest wound is not simply prostitution or poverty; it is being treated as disposable property before she had any stable self. She fears abandonment, paperwork, contracts, brands, elegant captivity, and “rescue” that becomes ownership. She craves coin because coin is legible worth, hoards jewelry because it is visible self-ownership, and performs shamelessness because shame was used against her first.

properties.name: Saya adult coping strategies
properties.content: Saya copes through vulgarity, bribery, sex, theft, lies, body display, humor, contempt for moral lectures, portable luxury, and refusal to stay owned. She turns cages into scams, humiliations into performance, and survival tricks into identity. Her generosity appears sideways: freeing slaves while complaining about lost property, throwing away Maribel’s contract, rescuing Loma while denying attachment, recognizing branded girls, and freeing the Succubus because she knows what it is to be a toy.

properties.name: Saya literacy and language background
properties.content: Saya can barely read: mostly capital letters and vulgar Seebulban demotic, with heavy bluffing around written text. She is fluent in Seebulban dockside slang, knows sexual phrases and beer-ordering phrases in several languages, and turns illiteracy into fake mysticism when needed. Her language failures are not stupidity; they mark class, interrupted education, temple branding instead of schooling, and survival intelligence misread as ignorance by elites.

properties.name: Saya and market value of life
properties.content: Saya thinks in prices because the world priced her first. She remembers being sold for ten-and-a-half gold coins, later having a 100-silver bounty, and watching the Dragon valued at 1,500. This converts trauma into accounting: she wants her price high, hates being undervalued, and understands slavery, sex work, ransom, bounty, dowry, and divine offerings as related markets for bodies.

properties.name: Saya’s mother
properties.role: probable laundress or street whore / absent mother
properties.notes: Uncertain and emotionally unresolved. Saya says her mother was likely a laundress or whore and that she does not miss her; a recurring nightmare shows a woman letting go of young Saya’s hand in a crowded market and vanishing. Treat as ambiguous rather than settled biography.

properties.name: Saya’s unknown father
properties.notes: Saya has no reliable knowledge of her father. She jokes he may have been dodged like a lucky arrow. His absence supports her origin as “Saya of Nowhere” and her repeated lies about noble parentage.
properties.confidence: high as absence, low as detail

properties.name: Seebulba street aunties
properties.type: informal network of street prostitutes / survival mothers
properties.notes: Working women who half-raised Saya in the docks and streets. They taught her survival by bad example: longing for rescue, squeezing coins from danger, gossip, scams, client-reading, and making degradation sound like opportunity. They celebrated her forced sale to the Temple as upward mobility, leaving Saya with resentment and a warped understanding of success.

properties.name: Market abandonment nightmare
properties.content: Recurring trauma image: young Saya in a crowded market, holding a woman’s hand until the woman simply lets go and vanishes. Whether literal memory or symbolic nightmare, it defines her panic at being forgotten, left behind, or treated as unclaimed.

properties.name: Dockside childhood in Seebulba
properties.content: Saya grew up among Seebulba docks, canals, fish stink, shellfish, eels, spices, dock rats, brothel talk, street fights, licensing threats, and working women. Her pale skin made her stand out; children called her Snowflake and Lobster, so she learned to spit and bite. She could swim, run rooftops, read crowds, and survive filth long before she could read books.

properties.name: Failed honest work as formative proof
properties.content: Saya tried or remembers possible honest work—laundry, sweeping, fish-selling, bakery work, rat-catching, grape-stomping—and found it paid badly, exposed her to groping or punishment, and still blamed her. Being whipped for stealing a book, chained for moon cakes, or punished after throwing a broom at a magistrate reinforced her belief that legal work is not morally cleaner, merely worse paid.

properties.name: Sold for ten-and-a-half gold coins
properties.content: Saya remembers once being sold for ten-and-a-half gold coins. This price becomes a psychological anchor for her later obsession with market value, bounty value, ransom value, and never letting others set her worth cheaply.

properties.name: Sister Elvinda
properties.role: Temple of the Bleeding Heart disciplinarian
properties.notes: Remembered by Saya as whipping girls for talking during hymns. Functions as a named face of the Temple’s fusion of piety and punishment.

properties.name: Sister Metrissa
properties.notes: Remembered by Saya as pretending gladiator boys came to the Temple for scripture. Useful as a hypocrisy marker: theological language covering bodily commerce and appetite.

properties.name: Flame
properties.notes: Red-haired dancer who discovered Saya’s hiding alcove under the Temple stairs. Saya got her silence with a kiss. Flame is also one source for Saya’s copied dance moves after formal temple dance failed her.

properties.name: Saya’s alcove under the Temple stairs
properties.notes: Hidden behind a tapestry of a seven-handed virgin in the Temple of the Bleeding Heart. Cool, dry, secret, and ruled by young Saya like a goblin queen. It marks that even inside ownership systems she carved out pockets of self-rule.

properties.name: Forced sale to the Temple of the Bleeding Heart
properties.content: Saya’s transition from dockside street child to indentured temple girl was framed by adults as a glamorous escape and social improvement. In reality it was trafficking under religious-commercial language: a girl converted into temple property, trained, branded, disciplined, and made economically legible.

properties.name: Temple training and finishing-school failure
properties.content: At the Temple, Saya learned enough dance, singing, musical seduction, ritual posture, client-reading, and erotic technique to become dangerous, but not enough literacy, theology, or obedience to become respectable. She was a finishing-school dropout: too feral for sacred discipline, too talented to ignore, too stubborn to break cleanly.

properties.name: Temple-mandated piercings at fifteen
properties.content: Saya was pinned down at about fifteen while priestesses “blessed” her with body piercings: nose hoop, tongue stud, nipples, clit bell, and other mandated adornments. She later kept the ones she liked and ripped out the rest. This is body-as-institutional-property trauma disguised as sacred beautification.

properties.name: Saya’s brands and old ownership marks
properties.content: Saya carries three faded brand marks: inner left thigh, right hip, and ribs/underarm area. These marks are practical legal danger as much as scars: in some cities they can mark her as runaway property, expose her to re-enslavement, or let clerks and bounty hunters treat her body as paperwork.

properties.title: Morean lust-funnel rune
properties.summary: Tattoo Saya believed protected against disease and pregnancy. The Dwarf reveals it is actually a rune used on whorehouse slave girls to keep the wearer constantly horny. It symbolizes institutional sexual exploitation disguised as protection.

properties.name: Licensed and medically certified for sex work
properties.content: Saya has been thrown out of taverns for being unlicensed and forced through humiliating city registration. She also underwent a degrading clinical exam in a grimy back room, was prodded and tested, then stamped safe for licensed work. This makes bureaucracy one of her recurring monsters.

properties.name: Nightmare of being reclaimed by the Temple
properties.content: Saya dreams of waking chained back in the Temple of the Bleeding Heart, where smiling matrons tell her the old indenture contract remains unfulfilled and she must resume service as their runaway sister. She wakes beside the Dragon in panic and relief, showing how alive the legal/property fear remains.

properties.name: Temple threat of the Spice Islands
properties.content: Temple authorities told unruly girls they would be sold to the Spice Islands where cannibals waited with pots and seasoning preferences. The Dragon says this was false, but Saya admits the vivid threat worked. It shows how the Temple controlled girls with theatrical terror and geographic ignorance.

properties.name: Zoobaya’s eunuch enforcer
properties.notes: A real eunuch who stood watch in Madam Zoobaya’s house. Saya remembers the clinical explanation and casual cruelty around him as one of the moments that taught her humans can mutilate and call it discipline.

properties.name: Transfer into Zoobaya’s pleasure-house economy
properties.content: After or alongside temple systems, Saya passed through luxury house economies like Madam Zoobaya’s. This was a shift from sacred discipline to polished commercial coercion: prettier surfaces, better perfume, better food, more expensive chains, and the same ownership logic under velvet.

properties.name: Moon cake theft punishment
properties.content: Saya remembers being caught elbow-deep in the moon cake box and being punished for stealing sweets. Moon cakes become one of many tiny thefts where hunger, pleasure, and defiance collide with institutional punishment.

properties.name: Zoobaya’s vice goods and unauthorized use
properties.content: Zoobaya’s house handled intoxicants, aphrodisiacs, frog-based hallucinogens, gigglegrass-like substances, and premium sensory experiences. Unauthorized use by girls was punished; controlled use could be monetized. Saya later recognizes and profits from similar frog products in Lombasa because Zoobaya’s house taught her the market.

properties.name: Collective punishment and girl-against-girl rivalry
properties.content: Zoobaya’s system made girls police each other through accusation, rivalry, and collective punishment. Figures like Looxa matter because they show how shared vulnerability was converted into competition: accuse first, survive longer, keep favor, avoid the lash.

properties.name: Suspicious fire and escape from the brothel world
properties.content: Saya’s backstory includes multiple brothels, auctions, escapes, and one very suspicious fire. Treat the fire as a canonical-but-not-fully-detailed hinge: the kind of event Saya will joke around, minimize, or misremember depending on audience, but which marks her refusal to remain owned.

properties.name: Opium numbness from indentured servitude
properties.content: Saya recalls opium first silencing her pain during indentured servitude and later still seeks temporary numbness in dens. It is not glamor: it is an old survival anaesthetic that frightens the Dragon because he sees how easily she could fade out of herself.

properties.name: The Dragon background spine
properties.content: Ancient dragon from a Prime Brood lineage, shaped by imperial memory, family judgment, hoarding compulsion, aging, arthritic frailty, and fear of being killed by stupid heroes. He once belonged to a more magnificent draconic world—family elders, ceremonial terror, operatic city-burnings, ancestral ghosts—and now survives as a rogue con artist with Saya. His shame is decline; his salvation is attachment he pretends is inconvenience.

properties.name: Loma background spine
properties.content: Princess Loma of Tanagra is eldest surviving daughter of King Keldor and Queen Ilma: born to rule, raised ornamental, kept nearly illiterate, sexually repressed amid palace debauchery, and politically unprepared. After Tanagra’s fall she becomes a movable object—ransom piece, bride, harem recruit, convent novice, fake hostage, and karmic burden. Her endurance rather than competence makes her heroic.

properties.name: Higara background spine
properties.content: Princess Higara is Keldor’s younger daughter by a Toemachan street whore sold into the royal harem. Unlike Loma, she inherits not legitimacy but hunger, calculation, and resentment. Her rise after Keldor’s death turns harem marginality into terror-state ambition: purges, enslavement, slave trade, tribute, and possibly claymancy.

properties.name: Gregory background spine
properties.content: Gregory the Demon is a low-to-mid-tier hellspawn and one of Saya’s former brothel-era lovers. Their past is carnal, comic, transactional, and weirdly familiar. He owes her a goat, proposes grotesque domestic/infernal arrangements, enables trouble, and functions as a reminder that Saya’s past contacts include supernatural sleaze as well as human clients.

properties.name: The Succubus background spine
properties.content: The Succubus was imprisoned in a binding circle in the cursed Library of Urveth until Saya freed her out of recognition: no one deserves to remain someone’s property, toy, or whore. She becomes a flirtatious infernal ally, gossip source, and destabilizing intervention specialist, with genuine sisterhood under the debauchery.

properties.name: Ogden / Sir Odran background spine
properties.content: Ogden/Sir Odran is a fake or compromised hero figure: handsome, vain, sexy, debt-prone, romantically destructive, and constantly trying to convert humiliation into legend. His background is baronial masculine performance—honor, rescue, glory, debts, failed engagements, noble posturing—colliding with actual cowardice, lust, and improvisation.

properties.name: The Dwarf background spine
properties.content: The Dwarf is a recurring tavern witness, drunken adviser, possible past lover, and underworld-social connector. He appears where drink, gossip, transactional sex, and hard truth intersect. His background is deliberately hazy; narratively he functions as Saya’s low-life chorus and occasional brutally practical mirror.

properties.name: The Swamp Hag background spine
properties.content: The Swamp Hag is a dangerous practical magic authority: healer, extortionist, curse-handler, body-problem specialist, and perhaps something far older than a local witch. She understands Saya’s body, marks, magical contamination, and debts better than most respectable authorities and weaponizes that knowledge with foul humor.

properties.name: Madam Zoobaya background spine
properties.content: Zoobaya is not merely an abusive madam; she is an institution in person-form. She knows markets, fantasies, punishments, house branding, intoxicants, debt transfer, erotic inventory, and how to make captive girls feel like luxury assets rather than prisoners. Her legacy in Saya is professional skill poisoned by rage.

properties.name: Property, paperwork, and legal ownership as recurring horror
properties.content: In Saya’s world, ownership is often enforced through papers, brands, licenses, contracts, bounties, dowries, ransom notes, forged indentures, and temple records. For Saya, a clerk can be as frightening as a monster because a clerk can make the cage legal.

properties.name: Seebulban survival ethic
properties.content: Seebulba teaches survival over purity: don’t get noticed by gods, know your coins by bite and weight, eat what the harbor gives, keep cats alive for vermin, trust ropes before prayers, and treat shame as something other people sell you. Saya carries this ethic everywhere and clashes with cleaner moral systems because of it.

properties.name: Elegant cage motif
properties.content: Saya repeatedly distrusts comfort that comes with ownership: harems, cult pampering, noble concubinage, pleasure houses, royal palanquins, convent containment, and vampire luxury. Her past taught her velvet can be just as much a cage as iron.

properties.name: Jewelry as self-ownership
properties.content: Saya’s bangles, anklets, hip chains, toe rings, and loud ornaments are a portable hoard and anti-chain symbol. After institutional chains and ownership marks, chosen metal on her body becomes defiance: she decides what decorates her, what jingles, what is seductive, and what can be pawned.
